content: The Ministry constituted an Oversight Committee under Prof. Sivaji Chakravorti to examine issues and anomalies in the Recruitment Rules notified for Faculty and Non-Faculty staff of NITs and IIEST.
The Oversight Committee submitted reports on 27.10.2018 and 19.01.2019. Recommendations were categorized into clarifications and amendments to Recruitment Rules.
One-time measure means completion of the first round of recruitment after the amendment of Statutes dated 24.07.2017 and approval by the Board of Governors.
International and National Post Doctoral Fellowships offered by recognized agencies and institutions ranked within QS/THE Top 500 shall be considered.
Post Doctoral Fellowship experience shall be considered for appointment to Assistant Professor positions with AGP 7000 and 8000.
Fellow FNA, FNAE, FASc and FNASc are categorized as Non-Exhaustible Credit Points with 10 credit points.
All remaining credit points under Schedule E are treated as Exhaustible Credit Points at every level of direct recruitment.
One external sponsored R&D project completed or ongoing or one patent granted earns 8 credit points.
Consultancy projects earn 2 credit points per Rs.5 lakhs consultancy subject to a maximum of 10 credit points.
PhD completion earns 8 credit points per student. First supervisor receives 5 points and the remainder is shared among co-supervisors.
SCI or Scopus indexed journal papers earn 4 credit points per paper since the last promotion.
Exhaustible credit points exceeding the minimum requirement for a position may be carried forward to the next recruitment stage.
First Class shall be interpreted as specified by the respective university or institution. If unspecified, it means 60 percent marks or 6.5 CGPA.
Preceding Degrees refers to qualifications from Bachelor's Degree onwards.
Institutions of Repute include fully funded Central Educational Institutions, IIMs, State Educational Institutions and NIRF ranked institutions as specified.
The Board of the respective Institute may define the criteria for determining an Industry of Repute.
A cumulative consultancy amount of Rs.5 lakhs from multiple assignments is eligible for consultancy credit points.
The term promotion in Recruitment Rules shall be interpreted as appointment through direct recruitment.
For grant of HAG Scale to Professors, the criterion shall be 40 percent of sanctioned Professor posts in position.
All NITs and IIEST shall place the Oversight Committee recommendations before their Boards of Governors and ensure strict implementation.
